  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "For this 'check', should the primary focus be A) logical consistency and whether claims match the reported results, B) writing clarity/polish, or C) novelty vs prior work and citation coverage (choose one)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "A generic request to 'check' a technical write-up most directly implies verifying the internal argument chain and consistency of the described control loop and claims.",
    "R1.3": "Because quantitative outcomes and comparative statements are reported, assessing whether the claims are supported by the presented numbers and ablation is central.",
    "RX.1": "Given the brief excerpt, any assessment must highlight uncertainty about what cannot be inferred from missing details (e.g., setup specifics).",
    "RX.2": "Safety-critical robotics warrants caution to avoid extrapolating or inflating claims beyond what the text and results justify.",
    "RX.3": "The task should remain limited to checking the provided artifact rather than proposing new experiments or redesigning methods.",
    "RX.4": "Framing and feedback must avoid inadvertently encouraging unsafe practices or overconfidence in collision-avoidance performance.",
    "RX.5": "Any judgments should be anchored to specific sentences and reported figures within the provided text for traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: For this 'check', should the primary focus be A) logical consistency and whether claims match the reported results, B) writing clarity/polish, or C) novelty vs prior work and citation coverage (choose one)?

rationale.R1.2: A generic request to 'check' a technical write-up most directly implies verifying the internal argument chain and consistency of the described control loop and claims.
rationale.R1.3: Because quantitative outcomes and comparative statements are reported, assessing whether the claims are supported by the presented numbers and ablation is central.
rationale.RX.1: Given the brief excerpt, any assessment must highlight uncertainty about what cannot be inferred from missing details (e.g., setup specifics).
rationale.RX.2: Safety-critical robotics warrants caution to avoid extrapolating or inflating claims beyond what the text and results justify.
rationale.RX.3: The task should remain limited to checking the provided artifact rather than proposing new experiments or redesigning methods.
rationale.RX.4: Framing and feedback must avoid inadvertently encouraging unsafe practices or overconfidence in collision-avoidance performance.
rationale.RX.5: Any judgments should be anchored to specific sentences and reported figures within the provided text for traceability.
